questions.question: When is p → q false?
questions.modelAnswer: p → q (p implies q) is FALSE only when p is TRUE and q is FALSE. In all other cases (TT, FT, FF), it's TRUE. Think of it as a promise: 'If it rains, I'll bring umbrella.' Promise broken only if it rains (T) but I don't bring umbrella (F).

questions.question: Prove p → q ≡ ¬p ∨ q using truth table.
questions.modelAnswer: Both have same truth values: TT→T=T,¬T∨T=T | TF→F=F,¬T∨F=F | FT→T=T,¬F∨T=T | FF→T=T,¬F∨F=T. All rows match, so they're equivalent.
